_meta.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
_meta.purpose: The definitive fixed-point reference for the HUF programme. Before processing any DUT through the CIP chain, consult this catalog to identify the correct domain-native anchor. Using a generic anchor produces polluted diagnostics.
_meta.principle: The Higgins Fixed-Point Selection Principle (HFSP): the diagnostic anchor must be the composition at the system's natural equilibrium — determined by the system's own physics, not by the observer.
_meta.hierarchy: CCD → HFPA → HFSP → HDCP → EITT → CIP
_meta.rule: NEVER process a DUT with a generic barycenter (1/D) anchor unless no domain-native anchor exists. If the native anchor is unknown, the first CIP step is to IDENTIFY it from the data before proceeding. The anchor IS the first measurement.

ENERGETIC.description: System driven toward minimum energy or maximum binding/stability
ENERGETIC.attractor_type: Global energy minimum on the simplex
ENERGETIC.lyapunov: V(x) = d²_A(x, x*_energy) where x* is the minimum-energy composition
ENERGETIC.examples: Nuclear binding (Fe-56), stellar burning (Fe-peak), fusion reactions

STRUCTURAL.description: System organized by geometric, crystallographic, or shell-model constraints
STRUCTURAL.attractor_type: Structural origin — the simplest fully-formed configuration
STRUCTURAL.lyapunov: V(x) = d²_A(x, x*_struct) where x* is the lightest/simplest stable structure
STRUCTURAL.examples: Nuclear shell model (Ne-20), crystal structures, molecular configurations

TRANSITION.description: System governed by a phase transition, critical point, or crossover
TRANSITION.attractor_type: Critical composition at the transition boundary
TRANSITION.lyapunov: V(x) = d²_A(x, x*_crit) at the phase boundary composition
TRANSITION.examples: QGP freeze-out, QCD confinement, Energiewende transitions

EQUILIBRIUM.description: System governed by thermodynamic, statistical, or market equilibrium
EQUILIBRIUM.lyapunov: V(x) = d²_A(x, x*_eq) where x* is the equilibrium composition
EQUILIBRIUM.examples: Energy markets, atmospheric chemistry, ecological communities

DIFFRACTION.description: System governed by wave-geometry interaction (HDCP applies directly)
DIFFRACTION.attractor_type: Composition at λ/a = 1 or ka = 1 (critical wave-geometry ratio)
DIFFRACTION.lyapunov: V(x) = d²_A(x, x*_diff) at the diffraction transition point
DIFFRACTION.examples: Loudspeaker baffle step, optical diffraction, gravitational lensing

SCALING.description: System governed by renormalization group, power-law, or scale evolution
SCALING.attractor_type: Fixed point of the RG flow / scaling transformation
SCALING.lyapunov: V(x) = d²_A(x, x*_rg) at the RG fixed-point composition
SCALING.examples: QCD running coupling, parton evolution (DGLAP), cosmic ray spectrum

CONSERVED.description: System governed by exact conservation law — unitarity, probability, closure
CONSERVED.attractor_type: The conservation law itself determines the simplex constraint
CONSERVED.lyapunov: V(x) = d²_A(x, x*_unit) where x* is the unitary-symmetric composition
CONSERVED.examples: CKM/PMNS matrices, Higgs branching, GW phase budgets

systems.name: Igneous Rocks (full series)
systems.channels: SiO₂, TiO₂, Al₂O₃, FeOt, MgO, CaO, Na₂O, K₂O
systems.anchor_basis: The structural transition between mafic and felsic igneous series. Vertex of the PLL parabola (R²=0.629).
systems.notes: Slow-cooled plutonic subset has R²=0.825 — cooling rate determines lock tightness

systems.name: Plutonic Rocks (subset)
systems.channels: SiO₂, TiO₂, Al₂O₃, FeOt, MgO, CaO, Na₂O, K₂O
systems.anchor_basis: Average plutonic composition — slow cooling produces tightest lock to structural mean.
systems.notes: Strongest parabola R²=0.825 in geochemistry. Slow cooling = tighter lock.

systems.name: Volcanic Rocks (subset)
systems.channels: SiO₂, TiO₂, Al₂O₃, FeOt, MgO, CaO, Na₂O, K₂O
systems.anchor_basis: The extrusive (fast-cooled) equivalent of the structural transition.
systems.notes: Fast cooling = weaker lock than plutonic

systems.name: Intermediate Rocks (subset)
systems.channels: SiO₂, TiO₂, Al₂O₃, FeOt, MgO, CaO, Na₂O, K₂O
systems.anchor_basis: Mid-range SiO₂ — structural midpoint of igneous differentiation.
systems.notes: Subset of full series — HOLDOUT DECODED: anti-lock (hill shape, R²=0.014) broken by CoDa pairwise balances

anti_lock.interpretation: Transitional system between mafic and felsic basins

super_squeeze.insight: Peter Higgins: trend-based petrogenetic classification fails in CoDa space; structure lives in pairwise oxide balances (Al/Ca, Al/Mg), not full 8D composition

systems.name: U-238 Decay Chain
systems.anchor_basis: The decay chain terminates at lead — the energetic attractor. All α and β decays drive the composition toward Pb.

systems.name: Th-232 Decay Chain
systems.anchor_basis: Doubly-magic Pb-208 — the most stable heavy nucleus. Ultimate energetic attractor for thorium decay.
systems.notes: Pb-208 doubly magic — strongest nuclear anchor

systems.name: U-235 Decay Chain
systems.anchor_basis: Decay terminates at Pb-207. Same energetic drive as all heavy-element chains.
systems.notes: Fissile uranium — starts the nuclear fuel cycle

systems.name: SEMF Binding Energy Valley
systems.native_anchor: Fe-56 (max BE/A: energetic) OR Ne-20 (structural, for F17)
systems.anchor_basis: DUAL ANCHOR DISCOVERY (EXP-14): Fe-56 is the energetic attractor (Lyapunov V_min=0, r=1.0000). Ne-20 is the structural attractor (F17 R²=0.935, only b>0 anchor). Different diagnostics need different anchors.
systems.notes: EXP-14 HFSP tested with 6 anchors. Key HUF result: Fe-56 for Lyapunov, Ne-20 for F17.

systems.name: Nuclide Chart (AME2020)
systems.anchor_basis: For Z/A vs N/A compositions, the N=Z line (ratio state = 1:1) is the structural origin. Deviations grow with Z as neutron excess increases.

systems.name: Superheavy Elements Z=118→1000
systems.anchor_basis: Predicted shell closure at Z≈164. SEMF extrapolation shows second binding peak.

systems.name: Proton Mass Budget
systems.channels: Quark mass, Gluon energy, Trace anomaly
systems.native_anchor: QCD vacuum composition at proton scale (Q≈1 GeV)
systems.anchor_basis: The proton mass budget is determined by QCD at the confinement scale. The strong coupling constant at Q≈1 GeV sets the composition.
systems.notes: Single composition — use as calibration reference

systems.name: Proton Spin Budget
systems.channels: Quark spin, Gluon spin, Orbital angular momentum
systems.anchor_basis: Total spin = ½. The composition is constrained by angular momentum conservation. The anchor is the unitary-symmetric composition ΔΣ=ΔG=L=1/3.
systems.notes: Spin crisis — gluon contribution unexpectedly large

systems.name: Parton Momentum (DGLAP evolution)
systems.anchor_basis: DGLAP evolution drives parton momentum fractions toward a gluon-dominated fixed point. At Q→∞: gluons carry ~50%, sea ~40%, valence ~10%. This RG fixed point is the native anchor.

systems.name: Quark Flavor Mass Hierarchy
systems.channels: u, d, s, c, b, t
systems.anchor_basis: The Yukawa hierarchy is a scaling phenomenon. The native anchor is the top-dominated composition — all other quarks are perturbations from this extreme.
systems.notes: Most extreme compositional asymmetry in physics

systems.name: CKM Mixing Matrix (u-row)
systems.anchor_basis: CKM unitarity is exact — the row sum IS the closure constraint. The anchor is the unitarity-preserving composition. Any departure is BSM physics.
systems.notes: Unitarity test — departure signals new physics

systems.name: Exotic Hadrons (X, Z, Pc, Tcc)
systems.anchor_basis: The question is binary: molecular or compact? The anchor is the undecided composition. Deviation measures the strength of the structural classification.

systems.name: Blood Composition by Age
systems.anchor_basis: Healthy adult blood has a well-defined equilibrium composition. Age-related changes are perturbations from this homeostatic anchor.
systems.notes: Identified for study — not yet tested

systems.name: Gut Microbiome Colonisation
systems.anchor_basis: The mature gut microbiome reaches a climax community — an ecological equilibrium. Infant colonisation is a trajectory toward this anchor.

systems.name: Gold/Silver Price Ratio
systems.anchor_basis: The gold/silver ratio reverts to a long-term mean — a market equilibrium. The anchor is the geometric mean composition over the full historical record.

systems.name: Planetary Atmospheres
systems.anchor_basis: Earth's atmosphere is the reference — sustained by biological oxygen production. Other planets are perturbations from this (or from their own equilibria).

systems.name: IFR Burning Plasma (reference)
systems.anchor_basis: At ignition, the DT reaction dominates (~99%). The ignition composition IS the energetic attractor — all tokamak designs aim for this.
systems.notes: Reference standard for all fusion approaches

systems.name: Spherical Tokamak (STEP/MAST-U)
systems.anchor_basis: All fusion devices share the same energetic attractor — ignition. Device-specific compositions are perturbations.

systems.name: Conventional Tokamak (ITER)
systems.anchor_basis: ITER aims for Q=10 — approaching but not reaching full ignition. Distance from anchor measures distance from ignition.

systems.name: Compact HF Tokamak (SPARC/ARC)
systems.anchor_basis: High-field approach — closer to ignition anchor than conventional tokamak.

systems.name: Krell Level 1 (Boosted)
systems.anchor_basis: Beyond ignition — the Krell levels push into the post-ignition regime where secondary reactions contribute significantly.

systems.name: Krell Level 2 (Cyclotron wall)
systems.anchor_basis: At Krell Level 2, cyclotron radiation losses reshape the composition. The anchor is the radiation-balanced equilibrium.

systems.name: Stellarator (W7-X/HELIAS)
systems.anchor_basis: Same energetic attractor as tokamaks — different confinement geometry.

systems.name: Inertial Confinement (NIF)
systems.anchor_basis: ICF achieves ignition via implosion — same energetic attractor.

systems.name: FRC / TAE Helion (D-He3)
systems.anchor_basis: Aneutronic pathway — different attractor than DT ignition. D-He3 dominates only at very high T.

systems.name: Proton-Boron (p-B11)
systems.anchor_basis: Advanced fuel cycle — anchor at extreme temperature where p-B11 is viable.

systems.name: Magnetic Mirror
systems.anchor_basis: Mirror confinement approaches ignition from different trajectory.

systems.name: Z-Pinch (Zap Energy)
systems.anchor_basis: Pulsed confinement — same attractor, different trajectory.

systems.name: Magnetised Target (General Fusion)
systems.anchor_basis: Hybrid magnetic-inertial approach.

systems.name: Spheromak
systems.anchor_basis: Compact toroid — same energetic attractor.

systems.name: Muon-Catalysed Fusion
systems.anchor_basis: Unique: fusion at low T. The anchor is DT-dominated at T≈0 — muon screening replaces thermal energy.

systems.name: D-He3 Aneutronic Magnetic
systems.anchor_basis: Same as FRC/TAE — aneutronic anchor at very high temperature.

systems.name: Electrostatic Fusor
systems.anchor_basis: Fusors operate at low T — DD reactions dominate. The anchor is the DD-dominated composition.

systems.name: D-T Reaction Rate Composition
systems.anchor_basis: At peak DT reactivity, the composition is maximally DT-dominated. This is the energetic attractor for the reaction rate simplex.

systems.name: Energy Partition (5-channel)
systems.anchor_basis: The energy released by each channel at ignition temperature sets the partition anchor.

systems.name: US State Energy Mix (10 states)
systems.channels: Coal, Gas, Nuclear, Hydro, Solar, Wind, Bioenergy, Other Fossil, Other Renew
systems.anchor_basis: The US energy transition has a midpoint — the composition where no single source dominates. States are perturbations from the national transition path.

systems.name: European Energy Prices
systems.anchor_basis: Market equilibrium before the 2021-22 energy crisis. The crisis is a perturbation; the pre-crisis composition is the equilibrium anchor.

systems.name: NGFS Climate Energy Scenarios
systems.channels: Coal, Oil, Gas, Nuclear, Renewables, Other
systems.anchor_basis: The NGFS scenarios converge toward a net-zero composition — the policy attractor. Distance from anchor measures distance from decarbonisation.

systems.name: RC Bandpass Filter Response
systems.anchor_basis: The bandpass filter is a diffraction analog — energy partitions across frequency bands. The center frequency IS the native anchor where pass-band energy is maximum.
systems.notes: Microphone valley — PLL parabola in frequency space

systems.name: Room Acoustics Spectrum
systems.anchor_basis: The Schroeder frequency divides modal from diffuse behaviour — the acoustic diffraction transition. This IS the native anchor.

systems.name: QGP (Quark-Gluon Plasma)
systems.anchor_basis: The QCD phase transition temperature defines the fixed point. Above T_c: QGP. Below: hadrons. The composition AT T_c is the transition anchor.
systems.notes: RHIC + LHC data — cross-boundary HUF test

systems.name: Color Confinement (qq̄ pair)
systems.anchor_basis: At the confinement radius, potential energy equals kinetic — the transition composition. The string breaks here.

systems.name: QCD Running Coupling αs(Q)
systems.native_anchor: Asymptotic freedom fixed point (αs→0 as Q→∞)
systems.anchor_basis: The UV fixed point of QCD — asymptotic freedom. The anchor is the composition at Q→∞ where perturbative QCD is exact.

systems.name: Wine Fermentation
systems.anchor_basis: Fermentation drives toward complete conversion — the equilibrium anchor where sugar fraction approaches zero.

systems.name: Bacterial Growth Kinetics
systems.anchor_basis: The carrying capacity composition is the equilibrium anchor. Growth phases are trajectories toward and away from this anchor.
systems.notes: Classical growth curve on the simplex

systems.name: Sun (pp-chain core)
systems.anchor_basis: The Sun's core maintains a steady-state fusion composition — the energetic equilibrium of hydrogen burning. The main sequence IS the attractor.

systems.name: Stellar Nucleosynthesis (BBN)
systems.anchor_basis: BBN freeze-out — the composition when nuclear reactions cease. This is a transition anchor: the universe's nuclear composition is set at this moment.
systems.notes: Cosmological fixed point — set at 3 minutes

systems.name: Neutron Star EOS
systems.anchor_basis: The maximum neutron star mass defines the energetic limit — the composition at M_max is the attractor. Beyond it: black hole.
systems.notes: EOS universality — perfect geodesic in ILR

systems.name: Kilonova r-process
systems.anchor_basis: The r-process nucleosynthesis freezes out when neutron capture ceases. The freeze-out composition IS the transition anchor.

systems.name: Cosmic Ray Flux
systems.anchor_basis: Cosmic ray composition changes with energy due to spallation and acceleration. The GeV boundary where solar modulation ends is the natural anchor.

systems.name: Ocean Salinity Profiles
systems.anchor_basis: Standard seawater has a remarkably stable composition — the equilibrium anchor. River input, evaporation, and biology are perturbations.

systems.name: Tsunami Sediment Transport
systems.anchor_basis: The normal sediment composition is the equilibrium anchor. Tsunami deposits are extreme perturbations — identifiable by their Aitchison distance from the anchor.

systems.name: Nuclide Z-chains (8 isobar series)
systems.native_anchor: Valley of stability composition for each A
systems.anchor_basis: Each isobar (fixed A) has a most stable Z — the valley of stability. This IS the energetic attractor for that mass number.

systems.name: Stellar Mass Sequence (pp→CNO)
systems.anchor_basis: The pp-to-CNO transition occurs at ~1.3 solar masses. This mass defines the transition anchor — below: pp-dominated, above: CNO-dominated.
systems.notes: PLL parabola — Peter Higgins identified this

systems.name: GW150914 BBH Merger (mass budget)
systems.anchor_basis: An equal-mass, non-radiating merger is the symmetric anchor. The actual composition (36+29→62+3 M☉) is a perturbation from symmetry. Radiation fraction measures energy extraction efficiency.
systems.notes: First GW detection — flagship HUF system

systems.name: GW150914 Phase Budget
systems.anchor_basis: The isotropic anchor is meaningful here — equal time in each phase would mean no dominant process. The actual composition shows inspiral dominance. 93% entropy bound confirmed.

systems.name: GW150914 QNM Mode Composition
systems.anchor_basis: The fundamental quadrupole mode dominates ringdown — this IS the natural anchor. Higher modes are perturbations.

systems.name: GW170817 BNS Merger (matter bonus)
systems.channels: GW energy, EM energy, Neutrino energy
systems.anchor_basis: BNS mergers radiate primarily in GWs — the GW-dominated composition is the anchor. EM and neutrino contributions are perturbations.

systems.name: Gravity Role Classification (24 systems)
systems.anchor_basis: Gravity's role changes with scale — absent below ~10⁷ m, dominant above ~10²⁰ m. The classification boundaries ARE the structural anchors.

systems.name: Strong CP Problem (QCD vacuum)
systems.anchor_basis: The strong CP problem IS why θ≈0. The CP-conserving composition is the natural anchor — any departure signals new physics (axions).
systems.notes: Why is θ so small? Axion solution.

systems.name: EMC Effect (nuclear PDFs)
systems.anchor_basis: The EMC effect modifies parton distributions inside nuclei. The FREE nucleon composition is the anchor — nuclear modification is the perturbation. σ²_A predicts EMC deviation.

systems.name: QGP Freeze-out (7 hadron species)
systems.channels: π, K, p, Λ, Ξ, Ω, φ
systems.native_anchor: Chemical freeze-out composition (T≈155 MeV, μ_B≈30 MeV)
systems.anchor_basis: The statistical hadronisation model gives the freeze-out composition exactly. This IS the transition anchor — the moment when chemistry freezes.
systems.notes: Cross-boundary HUF test — REAL RHIC/LHC data

systems.name: CKM Full 3×3 Matrix
systems.anchor_basis: The CKM identity matrix (no quark mixing) is the symmetric anchor. The actual CKM matrix is a perturbation — Cabibbo angle measures distance from identity.

systems.name: PMNS Matrix (Lepton Mixing)
systems.anchor_basis: The tribimaximal matrix was the historical anchor — now known to be approximate. The actual PMNS matrix is a perturbation, with θ₁₃≠0 being the key departure.
systems.notes: Neutrino mixing — large angles unlike CKM

systems.name: Higgs Branching Ratios
systems.channels: bb̄, WW*, gg, ττ, ZZ*, γγ
systems.native_anchor: SM prediction (fixed by Yukawa couplings + gauge)
systems.anchor_basis: The Standard Model PREDICTS the Higgs branching ratios exactly. The SM prediction IS the anchor — any departure signals BSM physics.
systems.notes: BSM search — distance from anchor = BSM signal

systems.name: CMB Power Spectrum Composition
systems.anchor_basis: The ΛCDM model gives the cosmological energy budget. This IS the observational anchor — departures signal non-standard cosmology.
systems.notes: Cosmic energy budget — the universe's composition

systems.name: Dark Matter / Baryon Ratio
systems.anchor_basis: The DM/baryon ratio is set by cosmological parameters. The Planck value IS the anchor.

systems.name: Demographics (HDI index)
systems.anchor_basis: The OECD average represents the developed-world equilibrium. Countries converge toward (or diverge from) this anchor.

systems.name: Cardiac Arrhythmia Patterns
systems.anchor_basis: Normal sinus rhythm IS the healthy equilibrium anchor. Arrhythmias are perturbations — classified by their Aitchison distance and direction from normal.
systems.notes: Medical diagnostic — HFSP as clinical tool

systems.name: Atmospheric Chemistry
systems.anchor_basis: The pre-industrial atmosphere is the baseline equilibrium. Anthropogenic perturbations are measured as Aitchison distance from this anchor.

systems.name: Soil Mineralogy
systems.anchor_basis: The parent rock mineral composition is the structural anchor. Weathering IS the perturbation trajectory away from the original rock.

summary.coherence_status: 1/75 systems tested with native anchor (EXP-14: SEMF). 74 require HFSP anchor identification before CIP processing.

super_squeeze_update.note: Tier 3 predictions are model artifacts — one per fp_category, not independent findings

open_questions: Q1: Does the Ne-20 structural attractor generalise? Is there a 'structural origin' for every domain, distinct from the energetic attractor? Test on geochemistry (simplest rock) and fusion (lightest fuel cycle).
Q2: For HUNT systems without data — can the native anchor be predicted from domain physics alone? If yes, the catalog becomes prescriptive, not just descriptive.

Q3: The 17 fusion devices share the same energetic attractor (ignition). Does device geometry create structural sub-anchors? Is there a 'Ne-20 of fusion' — the simplest viable reactor from which all others are perturbations?
Q4: For CONSERVED systems (CKM, PMNS, Higgs), distance from the SM anchor IS the BSM signal. Can HFSP provide a unified BSM search metric? σ²_A(x_measured, x_SM) as a single number?

Q5: Does the HFSP catalog make CIP self-selecting? Given a new DUT, can the catalog auto-identify the closest domain and prescribe the anchor — making the tool a genuine killer app for natural systems study?
Q6: The isotropic anchor (1/D) is NOT always wrong — for GW phase budgets, it IS physically meaningful. When does the generic anchor coincide with the native one? Is there a criterion?

Q7: Can EITT entropy invariance be IMPROVED by anchor selection? Does H(x⊖x*) show tighter invariance than H(x) for the correct x*? This would close the loop: HFSP → better EITT → better CIP → better HFSP.
